title: Wood Baby Gym
summary: A simple two-arch wooden baby gym cut entirely from one sheet of plywood, with a center bar for hanging toys.
description:
This whole gym comes from a single sheet of plywood, laid out efficiently so both arches and the center crossbar nest together with minimal waste — plan your cuts before you start rather than cutting piece by piece, since a layout done in one pass wastes far less material than one figured out as you go.

Cutting the two matching arches is the one place accuracy really matters: cut the first freehand with a jigsaw, then use it as a template to trace and cut the second, and clamp both together to sand the edges smooth and even in one pass. Matched by hand like this, the two arches end up reading as a mirrored pair even though neither was ever measured to a fraction of an inch.

tools.note: A scrolling blade makes the arch cuts easier to control.
Clamps the two arch pieces together for a matched sanding pass.

materials.name: Baby toys, with attachment loops or ribbon

steps.title: Lay out the cuts
steps.body: Lay out all the pieces on the plywood sheet as shown in your reference diagram before cutting anything — this is a small sheet supplying two arches and a crossbar, so an efficient layout matters. Take care as you cut.

steps.title: Cut the arches
steps.body: Lay out the arch curve on one piece and cut it carefully with a jigsaw, using a scrolling blade for better control on the curve. Once you have one arch cut cleanly, trace it onto the second blank and use it as a pattern for the second cut. Clamp both arch pieces together and sand the edges smooth and even in one pass.

steps.title: Cut the center piece
steps.body: Lay out the final center crossbar piece and cut it out with a jigsaw. Sand thoroughly — this is the piece little hands will hold onto most.

steps.title: Assemble the gym
steps.body: Attach the center piece to the two outside arch pieces with 2" screws.

steps.title: Hang the toys
steps.body: Attach the baby toys to the center crossbar using their attachment loops or a short length of ribbon, spacing them so they hang within easy reach.
materials: Baby toys, with attachment loops or ribbon

images.alt: Simple wooden baby gym with two arches and hanging toys
